name: Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular desde Soria
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Monte de Valonsadero traverse a diverse landscape of nearly 3,000 hectares, featuring extensive rebollo and pine forests, open meadows, and river valleys along the Duero and Pedrajas rivers. The area includes varied terrain with both gentle slopes and challenging climbs to panoramic viewpoints like La Cabaña and Los Castillejos. A dedicated bike path also connects the recreational area directly to the city of Soria, enhancing accessibility for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Monte de Valonsadero The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular desde Soria, a 30.8 miles (49.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete, offering a varied journey from Soria to the reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Hilario Necropolis loop from Circuito natural, a moderate 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route winds through the monte's diverse terrain, passing historical sites and natural features. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Circuito natural, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail leading through reforested pine forests, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Monte de Valonsadero is defined by rebollo forests, river valleys, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Monte de Valonsadero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Monte de Valonsadero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Monte de Valonsadero?
A: Monte de Valonsadero offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with nearly 100 different options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Monte de Valonsadero?
A: You can expect a diverse terrain featuring extensive rebollo (oak) and pine forests, open meadows, and river valleys along the Duero and Pedrajas rivers. Routes include both gentle slopes and more challenging climbs to panoramic viewpoints, as well as winding paths through reforested areas.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Monte de Valonsadero has several easy touring cycling routes, with 12 options specifically graded as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19884139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Circuito natural&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.0 miles (16.1 km) long and leads through reforested pine forests, making it a great choice for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 43 difficult touring cycling routes in Monte de Valonsadero. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge Over the Duero – Church of Santo Domingo loop from Circuito natural&lt;/a&gt;, a 58.8 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I reach on a touring bike in Monte de Valonsadero?
A: High points within Monte de Valonsadero, such as La Cabaña and Los Castillejos, offer impressive panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural landscape. Many routes are designed to pass by or lead to these viewpoints, providing stunning views of the forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to see while cycling?
A: Yes, Monte de Valonsadero is rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4000052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Honda Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt;, which features ancient schematic cave paintings. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40318478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Hilario Necropolis loop from Circuito natural&lt;/a&gt;, specifically winds through the monte's diverse terrain, passing historical sites.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on the routes?
A: You'll encounter a variety of natural features, including extensive rebollo (oak) forests, scattered pine groves, large meadows, and river valleys along the Duero and Pedrajas rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3977256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Duero and Pedrajas Rivers&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful spot, and some routes follow the riverbanks, offering serene views.

Q: How can I access Monte de Valonsadero for cycling, and is there parking available?
A: Monte de Valonsadero is easily accessible, with a dedicated bike path connecting the recreational area directly to the city of Soria. For those arriving by car, there are various public use areas and parking spots, such as near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valonsadero Guard House&lt;/a&gt;, which also serves as a starting point for many routes.

Q: Can I rent a bike in Monte de Valonsadero?
A: Yes, for your convenience, the Monte's Interpretation Center offers bicycle rental services, making it easy to explore the area even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: Are there any good circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many routes in Monte de Valonsadero are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041649794/embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-circular-desde-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular desde Soria&lt;/a&gt;, a 49.6 km route offering a varied journey from Soria to the reservoir and back.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Monte de Valonsadero?
A: The touring cycling routes in Monte de Valonsadero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising its diverse terrain and scenic beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Monte de Valonsadero?
A: The diverse landscapes of Monte de Valonsadero make it enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with vibrant foliage in spring and mild weather in autumn. Summer can be warm, but the extensive forested areas provide shade on many routes.

They are the ruins of a fifteenth century castle that dominate the town of Hinojosa de la Sierra.
The ruins are more impressive from afar but it is worth going up for the views of the Duero riverbank and Laguna de la Serna in the rainy season.
The town also has the church of the Asunción de Nuestra Señora right next to the castle and a little further away is the Renaissance palace of the Hurtado de Mendoza.
